|  | During the first 4 weeks of your program, your diet is simple in nature. After each training session your next 4 meals are training day meals. |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  | After that all your meals are rest meals until your next training session. |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
|  |  |  |  | Don't worry about the carb:fat ratio yet. Just worry about getting your protein in and hitting your calories on training days. |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| Training meals | Daily total | Per meal |  | Your rest/cut days are protein sparing modified fasts (PSMFs). |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| Protein Intake | 178 | 44.5 |  | This means you should aim to minimize caloric intake. |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| Kcal | 1726.6 | 431.65 |  | This effectively means your calories are maxima and your protein intake is a minimum. |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
|  |  |  |  | For satiety, it tends to help to either keep net carbs under 10 g per meal or to minimize fat intake. |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| Rest meals | Daily total | Per meal |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| Protein Intake | 153 | 38.25 |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| Kcal | 1484.1 | 371.025 |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |

|  | Meal: Phase II |  |  |  |  |  | You have a considerable degree of leeway with your macros on training/bulk days and with your breakfast on cut/rest days. |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Training Days (3x) | Breakfast | Lunch | Pre-workout | Post-workout | Total |  | The following error margins are acceptable. |  |  |  |  |
| Protein (g) | 31 | 31 | 31 | 72 | 165 |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| Fat (g) | 20 | 20 | 20 | 53 | 113 |  |  | Protein | Fat | Carbohydrate | Calories |

| Kcal | 452 | 452 | 452 | 1125 | 2481 |  | Per meal | +50% | +/-80% | +/-50% | +/-20% |
| Rest Days (4x) | Breakfast | Lunch | Dinner | Pre-bed | Total |  | Your rest/cut days are protein sparing modified fasts (PSMFs). |  |  |  |  |
| Protein (g) | 45 | 30.6 | 30.6 | 61.2 | 167.4 |  | This means you should aim to minimize caloric intake. |  |  |  |  |

|  |  |  |  |  |  |  | Don't worry about the carb:fat ratio. |  |  |  |  |
|  |  |  |  |  |  |  | For satiety, it tends to help to either keep net carbs under 10 g per meal or to minimize fat intake. |  |  |  |  |

full_text:
# Case study diet design Bart novice + intermediate.xlsx

## Sheet: Diet Phase I

|  | During the first 4 weeks of your program, your diet is simple in nature. After each training session your next 4 meals are training day meals. |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  | After that all your meals are rest meals until your next training session. |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
|  |  |  |  | Don't worry about the carb:fat ratio yet. Just worry about getting your protein in and hitting your calories on training days. |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| Training meals | Daily total | Per meal |  | Your rest/cut days are protein sparing modified fasts (PSMFs). |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| Protein Intake | 178 | 44.5 |  | This means you should aim to minimize caloric intake. |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| Kcal | 1726.6 | 431.65 |  | This effectively means your calories are maxima and your protein intake is a minimum. |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
|  |  |  |  | For satiety, it tends to help to either keep net carbs under 10 g per meal or to minimize fat intake. |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| Rest meals | Daily total | Per meal |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| Protein Intake | 153 | 38.25 |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| Kcal | 1484.1 | 371.025 |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |

## Sheet: Diet Phase II

|  | Meal: Phase II |  |  |  |  |  | You have a considerable degree of leeway with your macros on training/bulk days and with your breakfast on cut/rest days. |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Training Days (3x) | Breakfast | Lunch | Pre-workout | Post-workout | Total |  | The following error margins are acceptable. |  |  |  |  |
| Protein (g) | 31 | 31 | 31 | 72 | 165 |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| Fat (g) | 20 | 20 | 20 | 53 | 113 |  |  | Protein | Fat | Carbohydrate | Calories |
| Carbohydrate (g) | 37 | 37 | 37 | 90 | 201 |  | Daily total | +50% | -3% / + 50% | -50% | +/-1% |
| Kcal | 452 | 452 | 452 | 1125 | 2481 |  | Per meal | +50% | +/-80% | +/-50% | +/-20% |
| Rest Days (4x) | Breakfast | Lunch | Dinner | Pre-bed | Total |  | Your rest/cut days are protein sparing modified fasts (PSMFs). |  |  |  |  |
| Protein (g) | 45 | 30.6 | 30.6 | 61.2 | 167.4 |  | This means you should aim to minimize caloric intake. |  |  |  |  |
| Kcal | 456 | 296.82 | 296.82 | 593.64 | 1643.28 |  | This effectively means your calories are maxima and your protein intake is a minimum. |  |  |  |  |
|  |  |  |  |  |  |  | Don't worry about the carb:fat ratio. |  |  |  |  |
|  |  |  |  |  |  |  | For satiety, it tends to help to either keep net carbs under 10 g per meal or to minimize fat intake. |  |  |  |  |
